summary: Completed security code review of static Saudi visa website. Verified XSS protections, security headers, file access restrictions, and sanitization functions. Found minor issues with console statements in production code.

security_issues.issue: console.error statements still present in production code
security_issues.description: console.error and console.log statements found in production files, should be removed for production deployment

passed_tests: ✅ sanitizeHTML function exists and works correctly in /app/frontend/public/script.js
✅ sanitizeForHTML function exists and works correctly in /app/frontend/public/professions.js
✅ createBlogCard uses sanitized variables (safeSlug, safeTitle, safeImage, safeAuthor, safeDesc)
✅ createProfessionCard uses sanitizeForHTML and showProfessionByIndex instead of JSON.stringify in onclick
✅ showError and showSuccess use sanitized messages
✅ highlightText uses escapeRegExp to prevent ReDoS
✅ netlify.toml has Content-Security-Policy, HSTS, Permissions-Policy headers
✅ _redirects blocks access to sensitive files (.env, .git, .gitignore, .md files)
✅ _redirects allows robots.txt and sitemap.xml BEFORE blocking wildcards
✅ netlify.toml redirects block professions_processed.json and design_guidelines.json
✅ printProfessionDocument in professions.js uses safeName and safeCode
✅ No raw user data is passed to innerHTML without sanitization
action_item_for_main_agent: Remove console.error and console.log statements from production JavaScript files: /app/frontend/public/script.js:114, /app/frontend/public/professions.js:44, /app/frontend/public/service-worker.js:21
